title: AN ACT AMENDING REPUBLIC ACT NO. 9718. ENTITLED: "AN ACT CONVERTING THE NAVAL INSTITUTE OF TECHNOLOGY IN THE MUNICIPALITY OF NAVAL, PROVINCE OF BILIRAN INTO A STATE UNIVERSITY TO BE KNOWN AS THE NAVAL STATE UNIVERSITY, INTEGRATING THEREWITH THE BILIRAN NATIONAL AGRICULTURAL COLLEGE IN THE MUNICIPALITY OP BILIRAN AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR"

chunks.text:
115 OG No. 5, 1178 (February 11, 2019); Manila Bulletin, January 31, 2019

[ REPUBLIC ACT NO. 11170, January 03, 2019 ]

AN ACT AMENDING REPUBLIC ACT NO. 9718. ENTITLED: "AN ACT CONVERTING THE NAVAL INSTITUTE OF TECHNOLOGY IN THE MUNICIPALITY OF NAVAL, PROVINCE OF BILIRAN INTO A STATE UNIVERSITY TO BE KNOWN AS THE NAVAL STATE UNIVERSITY, INTEGRATING THEREWITH THE BILIRAN NATIONAL AGRICULTURAL COLLEGE IN THE MUNICIPALITY OP BILIRAN AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR"

Be if enacted by the Senate and House of Representatives of the Philippines in Congress assembled:

SECTION 1. Section 1 of Republic Act No, 9718 is hereby amended to read as follows:

"SECTION 1.

Renaming of the Naval. State

University.

The Naval State University in the Municipality of Naval. Province of Biliran is hereby renamed as the Biliran Province State University (BiPSU), hereinafter referred to as the University, integrating therewith the Biliran National Agricultural College in the Municipality of Biliran, both located in the Province of Biliran. The main campus of the University shall continue in its present location in the Municipality of Naval, Province of Biliran."

"(j) The President of the federation of alumni associations of the University, member: and

"(k) Two (2) prominent citizens from the private sector who have distinguished themselves '

r

in their professions or fields of specialization of the University, members.

"The Board shall appoint the two (2) prominent citizens from among a list of at least five (5) qualified persons in the Province of Biliran, whose nominations were submitted to the Board after publication for at least two (2) consecutive weeks, as recommended by the search committee constituted by the University President, in consultation with the Chairperson of the CHED and the other members of the Board, based on the normal standards and qualifications for the position set by the Board.

"xxx."

SEC. 3. Section 9 of Republic Act No. 9718 is hereby amended to read as follows:

"SEC. 9.

The University President. -

The University shall be headed by a President, who shall render full-time service. The President shall be appointed by the Board, subject to the guidelines, qualifications and/or standards sec by the Board, upon recommendation of a duly constituted search committee:

Provided,

That in order to effect a smooth transition, the incumbent OIC President of the Naval State University shall serve as the first and full-fledged and duly appointed President of the University whose term is considered as a transition period for four (4) years.

SEC. 4. Section 10 of Republic Act No. 971S is hereby amended to read as follows:

"SEC. 10.

Beads of University Campuses. -

Campus Directors shall head the University campuses and they shall render full-time service. They shall be appointed by the University President, subject to the guidelines, qualifications and standards set by the Board:

Provided.

That the incumbent college administrators of the extension campuses shall serve as Campus Directors of their respective campuses.'"

SEC. 5.

Implementing Rules and Regulations.

- The Governing Board of the BiPSU, in consultation with the CHED, shall formulate the guidelines to fully implement the provisions of this Act within ninety (90) days from its effectivity.

SEC. 6.

Effectivity. -

This Act shall take effect, fifteen 05) days after its publication in the

Official Gazette

or in a newspaper of general circulation.

Approved,

(SGD.)

VICENTE C. SOTTO III

President of the Senate

(SGD.)

GLORIA MACAPAGAL-ARROYO

Speaker of the House of Representatives

This Act which originated in the House of Representatives was passed by the House of Representatives and the Senate on October 10, 2018.

(SGD.)

MYRA MARIE D. VILLARICA

Secretary of the Senate

(SGD.)

DANTE ROBERTO P. MALING

Acting Secretary General House of Representatives

Approved: JAN 03 2019

(SGD.)

RODRIGO ROA DUTERTE

President of the Philippines
